pages.raw_text:
Mr.. Nelson of Chester, WASH,
formerly Of Iowa, the man who
received first prize for Wagner
apples at the National Apple
show of 1908, has purchased 10
acres Of fine apple land in
Washtucna valley. Mr.. Nelson
spent two days looking over the
valley and has joined the ranks
of Washtucna enthusiasts. He
will plant the tract to standard
winter apples this fall. Mr.. Burt
OF Spokane also bought IL acres
the same day. They are salts
fled and so are the many other
buyers. Go and look this valley
over and ask the men who are
there why they bought In Wash
tucna valley. They know and
will be glad to tell you that they
bought because they found 1t,
after careful investigation, to-
be the best fruit valley in the
state under development at the
present time. Now is the time
to buy, while prices range from
$3.50 to $3.50 per acre, and easy
terms are possible.
THE PALOUSE IRRIGATION


& POWER co.


702 First Ave.
